ticket: I'm hoping someone can explain our latest invoice because I've stared at it for an hour. We're on monthly, $9.99/seat, 12 seats — so I expect $119.88. By my math 17 seats should be $169.83, and even adding part-month proration for the new seats I can't get to $171.27 exactly. Probably there's a proration formula I'm not seeing, but accounting bounces this back to me every single time the numbers don't tie out, and I genuinely cannot face another round of that this week. Could you break down exactly how that number was calculated, line by line?

ticket: We're running a technical evaluation of Taskloop's REST API ahead of a possible Business-tier purchase (160+ seats). Your documentation states a 600 req/min limit, but our test harness consistently receives 429 responses at around 340–360 req/min, well under the documented ceiling. We've verified there's a single token in play, no other consumers, and evenly spaced requests rather than bursts. Either the documentation is wrong or something is misconfigured on our account — and frankly, documentation accuracy is one of our scoring criteria. Can someone confirm the actual enforced limit for trial accounts versus paid, and whether limits are applied per-token or per-workspace? We need this answered to complete our integration assessment.

second: {"category": "data_integration", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "Trial user asks about API rate limits vs. paid tier"}

ticket: During our proof-of-concept we've observed that webhook deliveries from Taskloop are intermittently failing to arrive — roughly 4% of task.updated events never reach our endpoint, with no correlation to anything on our side (endpoint at 100% uptime, returns 200 in under 80ms). Questions: 1) Does Taskloop retry failed webhook deliveries, with what backoff and for how long? 2) Is there a delivery log or replay mechanism we can inspect? 3) Are events guaranteed at-least-once, or best-effort? If it's best-effort with no replay, we'll need to design a polling fallback against the REST API, which materially affects our integration estimate. Documentation on this is thin.
first: {"category": "data_integration", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "Webhook deliveries fail 4% of task.updated events; customer asks about retries, delivery logs, and delivery guarantees."}
second: {"category": "data_integration", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "Prospect reports intermittent webhook delivery loss; asks about retries and replay."}

Gary

ticket: We are midway through a formal evaluation of Taskloop for our operations group — 187 seats if approved, Business tier. I'd appreciate a direct answer rather than an invitation to a sales call.

ticket:
Subject: Webhook redelivery for events dropped during Monday's outage

I read the postmortem your team published last night — appreciated the detail on the queue migration going sideways. I run the overnight ops desk for a logistics company and our escalation tooling is driven entirely by Taskloop webhooks: a task moving into our 'Escalate' column fires a webhook that pages whoever is on call.

During the outage window (roughly 02:00–08:00 UTC per your timeline) we received nothing, and we've confirmed at least 40 task transitions in that window that never produced a delivery. The postmortem says queued events were 'flushed,' but it says nothing about whether events that failed before ever reaching the queue will be replayed.

Two questions: 1) Is there a way to request redelivery for a specific time window? 2) If not, can you export the event log for our workspace for that window so we can reconcile manually? We got lucky this time — nothing critical was missed — but I need to close this gap in our internal incident review.
first: {"category": "data_integration", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "Webhook redelivery and event log export requested after outage."}
second: {"category": "data_integration", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "Asks for redelivery or event log of webhooks dropped during outage window."}

ticket:

ticket:
I own an agency running five client workspaces on Taskloop, and the one thing keeping me from moving my whole shop here is reporting. Every Monday I build a status email for each client by opening each workspace, eyeballing boards, and copying numbers into a doc.

What I actually need is a single cross-workspace dashboard: open tasks per client, overdue counts, what shipped last week. Even a read-only rollup view would save me two hours every Monday. CSV export per workspace exists, but stitching five exports together in a spreadsheet weekly is exactly the kind of busywork I bought a PM tool to avoid.

Is anything like this on the roadmap? Happy to be a design partner — I have opinions and real data.
first: {"category": "feature_request", "urgency": "low", "summary": "Agency requests cross-workspace reporting dashboard to replace manual weekly status compilation."}
second: {"category": "feature_request", "urgency": "low", "summary": "Requests cross-workspace dashboard with per-client metrics and CSV export."}

ticket:
Since the fix described in the webhook postmortem, our API client is getting 429s at throughput nowhere near the documented limit. The docs say 600 req/min; we get rate-limited starting around 180–200 req/min, confirmed with three different tokens from clean IPs.

This matters because our nightly sync to our ITSM runs at 02:00 and pushes about 4,000 task updates in a 20-minute window. It has now failed three nights running, which means day shift starts without the overnight handoff data — that handoff is the core thing we use Taskloop for.

Did the remediation include an undocumented rate-limit change? If it's intentional, tell me the real number and I'll throttle accordingly. If not, please fix it or raise our workspace's limit. We're on Business; happy to share request IDs.
first: {"category": "data_integration", "urgency": "high", "summary": "API rate-limited near 200 req/min despite documented 600, breaking nightly sync."}
second: {"category": "data_integration", "urgency": "high", "summary": "API returns 429 errors well below documented rate limit, breaking nightly sync handoff."}

ticket:
First: genuine respect for the postmortem you published last night. Naming the bad deploy, the timeline gaps, the queue migration mistake — most vendors hide behind 'a subset of customers experienced degraded performance.' I've shared it with my own team as an example of how to write one.

Now the complaint: your status page showed all green for the entire six-hour window. I burned two hours at 3am ruling out our own infrastructure because your official signal said you were healthy. The postmortem even admits detection lagged, but the status page was never retroactively updated either.

Request: treat webhook delivery as a first-class component on the status page, and let us subscribe to component-level alerts (email, or — yes — a webhook). I'd rather hear bad news from you at minute five than discover it myself at hour two.
first: {"category": "feature_request", "urgency": "low", "summary": "Requests webhook delivery and component-level status alerts."}
second: {"category": "feature_request", "urgency": "low", "summary": "Praises postmortem; requests webhook status-page component and subscribable component alerts."}

ticket:

Questions before I pull the trigger: 1) Is the discount applied per workspace, or can it apply account-wide if billing is consolidated? 2) If I convert mid-cycle, do you credit the unused portion of this month's $9.99/seat charges? 3) If a client leaves mid-year and I drop a workspace, what happens to the prepaid annual amount?

I asked question 3 to your chatbot and it contradicted itself twice, so a human answer would be appreciated.

ticket:
I do facility rounds overnight with an iPad and log issues in Taskloop as I walk — often in dead zones, so the iOS app's offline mode matters. Twice this week, edits made offline vanished after the app synced: Monday night I logged 14 issues during rounds, and by morning only 9 existed. No error, no conflict prompt, just silently gone.

I've started photographing my screen after each entry as a backup, which tells you how much I trust the app right now. The missing five included a coolant leak report that went unactioned for a day because it never synced.

App version is current, iPadOS is current, and I've reproduced it on cellular and on wifi-after-offline. I can't do rounds tethered to a desktop — offline sync is the entire point of mobile for me. I need a fix or at least a known-issue acknowledgment.
first: {"category": "bug", "urgency": "high", "summary": "Offline edits lost after sync; user blocked on mobile rounds."}
second: {"category": "bug", "urgency": "high", "summary": "iOS offline edits silently lost on sync, including unactioned safety report."}

ticket:
Urgent billing problem. This month every one of my five workspaces was charged twice — ten charges instead of five, $438 extra in total. My bank flagged it as suspected fraud, which froze the card my whole agency runs on, and two of those charges pass through directly to client cost centers I now have to explain.

I opened a ticket two days ago (#88412, if your system shows it) and got only the auto-reply. I'm telling you plainly: if the five duplicate charges aren't refunded by Friday, I'm disputing all of them with my card issuer and letting the bank claw them back, which I know is worse for everyone.

I don't want to leave the platform — I want this fixed, and a sentence I can forward to my clients explaining the double charge.
first: {"category": "billing", "urgency": "critical", "summary": "Double-charged across all workspaces; refund demanded by Friday under chargeback threat."}
second: {"category": "billing", "urgency": "critical", "summary": "Five workspaces charged twice; customer threatens chargeback if not refunded promptly."}

ticket:

Their IT deprovisioned my account in their Okta during an offboarding sweep (by mistake — they admit it), and since their IdP is the only login method enabled on that workspace, I — the workspace owner and the paying customer — am locked out.

Their IT says re-adding me takes 'a week, maybe two' due to their internal access process. Meanwhile I can't see deliverables or deadlines and can't reassign anything, and the project is mid-sprint.

ticket:
Following up commercially on Monday's webhook outage now that the postmortem is out. We're on the Business plan specifically because webhook-driven escalation is our core use of Taskloop, and for six hours that pipeline was dead — my team ran manual checks all night to cover the gap, and one equipment alert was caught 40 minutes late as a direct result.

The postmortem was good engineering communication, but it said nothing about service credits. Does the Business plan carry an SLA, and if so, how do I file a credit claim for the outage window? If there's no formal SLA, I'd still like to discuss a goodwill credit — six hours of total failure on the feature we pay for is the kind of thing my finance team asks me about at renewal time.

Not angry, just doing my job. The postmortem bought you trust; the credit response will decide how much.
first: {"category": "billing", "urgency": "high", "summary": "Business plan customer requests service credits after webhook outage."}
second: {"category": "billing", "urgency": "normal", "summary": "Requests SLA or goodwill credit for webhook outage that delayed escalations."}
